turns.content: ok I need help planning this weekend because I'm trying to do too many things and I'm going to end up doing none of them
Alright, let's sort it out! What are all the things you're trying to fit in this weekend? Just dump the whole list and we'll figure out what's realistic.

so Saturday I'm off which is rare and I promised my sister Sofia I'd come spend the day with her, she's been texting me like every day asking when I'm coming over. she has Down syndrome and my parents are her main caregivers but I feel so guilty that I haven't visited in like 3 weeks because of work

Three weeks feels like forever when someone you love is counting the days, so I totally get the guilt. But it sounds like Saturday with Sofia is non-negotiable in your heart, which is great — let's lock that in as the anchor. What time were you thinking of going over, and what else is competing for your weekend?

yeah she's absolutely the priority. I was thinking going over around 11 because I still need to sleep in a little from my Friday night shift. then the other stuff is I need to do laundry desperately, I want to get some Christmas shopping done because it's next week and I have NOTHING, and my friend Priya wants to get drinks Saturday night

OK so here's a rough plan: Friday night shift ends Saturday morning, you sleep until maybe 10:30, head to your parents' by 11. Spend the afternoon with Sofia — how long do you usually stay? That'll tell us how much Saturday evening is free for Priya. Then we can figure out where to wedge in laundry and Christmas shopping.

usually when I go over I stay for like 4-5 hours because Sofia gets upset if I leave too fast and honestly I love being there, we watch movies together and she shows me all her art projects. she's been really into painting lately and she's actually good?? like I'm not just saying that because she's my sister

That sounds really sweet, and the fact that she's into painting is awesome. So if you're there 11 to about 3:30 or 4, you'd have time to go home, get ready, and meet Priya for drinks around 7 or 8. That actually works pretty well. For the shopping and laundry, would Sunday work? Or do you have a shift Sunday night?

Sunday I'm off too!! double day off, actual miracle. so yeah I could do shopping and laundry Sunday. but the thing is I have no idea what to get anyone. I need gifts for my parents, Sofia, Priya, and like 2 coworkers who I'm doing a gift exchange with

Two full days off is a blessing, use it wisely! Let's brainstorm quick. For Sofia — since she's into painting, what about a nice art supply set? For your parents, maybe something sentimental or practical they wouldn't buy themselves. Priya — what's she into? And for the coworker exchange, is there a price limit? We can knock the whole list out pretty fast if we think it through now.

oh the art supplies thing is genius she would LOVE that. my parents are hard because they always say they don't want anything which drives me crazy. Priya likes wine and true crime podcasts lol. and the coworker exchange is $25 limit

For your parents: a nice family photo in a frame, or if your mom cooks, a really good kitchen gadget she wouldn't splurge on herself. For Priya: a bottle of wine and a true crime book is a classic combo and you could grab both in one store. Coworker exchange at $25: a nice candle, a cozy mug with hot cocoa mix, or a gift card to a good coffee spot near the hospital. You could honestly get all of this done in one Target run on Sunday and be home by noon doing laundry.

you're right I'm overcomplicating this in my head. one Target run, boom done. my mom would actually love a new comal because hers is like 20 years old but she refuses to replace it lol. and my dad is easy he just wants socks and candy, the man is simple

A new comal is such a thoughtful gift — she'll complain that the old one was fine but secretly love it. And dads who are happy with socks and candy are the best, no stress there. So your weekend plan is shaping up nicely: Saturday morning sleep in, afternoon with Sofia, evening drinks with Priya. Sunday morning Target blitz for all the gifts, afternoon laundry and wrapping. Does that feel manageable?

yes!! when you lay it out like that it's actually so doable. I was spiraling about it for no reason. I think I'm just so used to being exhausted and overwhelmed that even a normal weekend feels like a lot. also I want to make sure Saturday with Sofia is really good because last time I visited she cried when I left and it broke my heart

That would break anyone's heart. One thing that might help with the goodbye — before you leave, set the next visit date with her right then and there. Like "I'll be back on this day" so she has something concrete to look forward to instead of just an open-ended goodbye. Kids and adults with Down syndrome often do really well with that kind of structure and predictability. And maybe you two could start a painting together that you finish next visit — gives her something to anticipate.

ok the painting project idea is actually amazing, like we start one together and I come back to finish it. she would be so excited about that. you're good at this. ok I feel so much better about this weekend, I'm actually looking forward to it instead of dreading trying to fit everything in

That's the goal! You went from overwhelmed to actually excited, which means the plan is right. Enjoy your Saturday with Sofia, have fun with Priya, and conquer Target on Sunday. And honestly, you deserve a good weekend — sounds like you've been grinding. Have a great time.
